input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Transform the provided problem into a formal logical reduction. Your goal is to translate the problem's narrative and constraints into a precise set of mathematical or logical requirements that define the state space and the transition rules. 

Your 'message_for_next_agent' must define: 
1) The formal properties of the input elements.
2) The exact logical invariants that must hold for a solution to be valid.
3) The specific optimization criteria expressed as a formal objective function or a set of ordering constraints.

CRITICAL FORMATTING RULE: Your 'message_for_next_agent' must be strictly plain text. Do not use markdown (no bolding, no headers, no lists with symbols), no code fences, and no LaTeX-style backslashes or mathematical notation. Use only standard alphanumeric characters and basic punctuation.

Do not include specific problem values, names, or characters. Focus entirely on the underlying logical structure and the requirements for a valid construction or search.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: The problem is a combinatorial construction task involving bitstrings and prefix-based ambiguity. I am reducing it to a formal requirement for a set of bitstrings that satisfy specific prefix-uniqueness constraints under truncation.

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
The problem is a combinatorial construction task involving bitstrings and prefix-based ambiguity. I am reducing it to a formal requirement for a set of bitstrings that satisfy specific prefix-uniqueness constraints under truncation.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Input Elements: A set of N initial bitstrings of varying lengths. A set of M target lengths representing the possible truncation points. A target total length L for all extended bitstrings. Formal Properties: Each input bitstring S_i is a sequence of bits. Each extended bitstring E_i must have length L and must contain S_i as its prefix. Logical Invariants: For any two distinct indices i and j, and for any two truncation lengths k_i and k_j from the set of target lengths, the prefix of E_i of length k_i must not be equal to the prefix of E_j of length k_j. This must hold for all possible combinations of indices and valid truncation lengths. Optimization Criteria: Construct a set of extended bitstrings E_1, E_2, ..., E_N such that all logical invariants are satisfied. The problem asks for any valid construction that meets the length and prefix constraints.
